news.title: Odisha: 244 acres to be acquired for Jeypore Airport upgrade
news.para: The Odisha government is upgrading Jeypore Airport to Category 3C, requiring the acquisition of 244 acres. This expansion will allow the airport to handle 72-seater aircraft and operate 24/7, significantly boosting regional connectivity, economic growth, and tourism in southern Odisha.

news.title: Margao, Mormugao ODPs to be converted into masterplans
news.para: Goa's Town and Country Planning Department is set to transform existing development plans for Margao and Mormugao into advanced GIS-based masterplans. This initiative, part of the Centre's Amrut 2.0 program, will create digital blueprints for these cities. The process involves detailed data collection on various urban aspects. Experts anticipate the conversion will take over a year to complete.

news.title: Belagavi civic body's crackdown on illegal constructions raises accountability questions
news.para: Belagavi City Corporation intensified its drive against illegal constructions with a partial demolition of a four-storey building in Maruti Galli. The operation, while welcomed by some, has sparked controversy over alleged lack of notice and financial losses for tenants. Questions are also being raised about the corporation's monitoring and approval processes for such structures.

news.title: KHB plans a new residential layout on 92 acres in Belagavi
news.para: Karnataka Housing Board is planning a new residential project in Honnihal village. The scheme will be spread across 92 acres. This initiative aims to meet the growing housing needs of Belagavi city. Landowners will receive fair compensation and rehabilitation benefits. The government's approval is awaited. Public notification will follow the approval process.

news.title: Smart city mission is scrapped in Maharashtra, special purpose vehicles gone
news.para: Maharashtra government has dismantled Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs) for the Smart City Mission across eight cities, including Nagpur. Municipal commissioners will now assume CEO roles and all SPV assets will be transferred to municipal corporations. This move signals the closure of the decade-long urban flagship program, with responsibilities shifting to local civic bodies.

news.title: After bulldozers, focus shifts to cleanup, road restoration and infra upgrade in Gurugram
news.para: Gurugram authorities are now clearing debris and restoring roads after demolishing encroachments. The Town and Country Planning department has directed developers and the Municipal Corporation of Gurugram to complete restoration work within 15 days. This move also paves the way for much-needed infrastructure upgrades in colonies. Residents are observing the progress, hoping for lasting improvements and no re-encroachment.

news.title: MCM sets April 27 deadline for Manesar land survey
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Manesar has set April 27 as the deadline for its digital land survey under the Centre’s ‘Naksha’ project. This initiative aims to create a digital database of all land parcels, enhancing transparency and aiding in infrastructure development. Residents are urged to cooperate with survey teams to ensure accurate land records.

news.title: Mumbai: Dharavi residents to get 500-754 sq ft rehabilitation flats
news.para: Dharavi redevelopment project residents will now receive larger rehabilitation flats. The government has proposed a minimum of 500 sq ft carpet area, a significant increase from the previous offer. This change aims to benefit thousands of households. Residents await further details on relocation and the master plan.

news.title: Lavasa homebuyers file plea at NCLT against resolution
news.para: Homebuyers of Lavasa, India's first privately built hill town, have filed a fresh appeal. They allege serious flaws in the resolution plan approved by creditors. The homebuyers claim discriminatory treatment and a lack of transparency in the insolvency process. They seek intervention from the appellate tribunal to address these concerns and ensure fair treatment.

news.title: WeWork India leases over two lakh sq ft in Pune’s Wakad
news.para: WeWork India has leased over 204,000 sq ft of office space in Pune's Wakad locality for a long-term agreement. The flexible workspace provider will occupy five floors at Phoenix Millennium Tower 3, with monthly rents starting at ₹73 per sq ft. This expansion highlights the growing demand for managed workspaces in emerging Pune hubs.

news.title: Hospitals, hotels & residential buildings among 42 marked for flouting fire safety norms in Ghaziabad
news.para: Ghaziabad's fire department has declared 42 buildings, including residences and hospitals, 'unsafe' due to non-compliance with fire safety norms. Despite court proceedings initiated in 2017, only nine of the initially identified 51 buildings have upgraded their infrastructure. Recent inspections revealed ongoing issues with fire equipment in numerous residential complexes and commercial establishments.

news.title: Land auction in Hyderabad's Osman Nagar fetches ₹51 crore per acre
news.para: Hyderabad's realty market is booming as Osman Nagar witnessed record land auction bids, fetching Rs 51.25 crore per acre. This surge, driven by strategic location and multi-use zoning, signals strong investor confidence in Telangana's urban growth. Developers like Rajapushpa and Brigade Group are investing heavily, anticipating significant appreciation in this prime micro-market.

news.title: Regularisation of properties in unauthorised colonies in Delhi to begin from April 24
news.para: Residents of over 1,500 unauthorised colonies in Delhi can now apply online for property registration. The MCD's Swagam portal will facilitate this process. A new policy allows for the regularisation of these colonies. Over 10 lakh households stand to benefit. This digital system aims to streamline property ownership and registration for citizens.

news.title: Delhi: MCD orders survey to identify residential properties being used for commercial purposes
news.para: The Delhi Municipal Corporation is undertaking a rapid survey throughout the city. The focus is on pinpointing residences that have been converted for business purposes. Zonal teams will carry out on-site evaluations and gather corroborated information. This data will then be forwarded to the Supreme Court.

news.title: Swiss liftmaker Schindler's sales dip 5.1% on forex impact
news.para: Schindler reported a 5.1% drop in first-quarter sales, primarily due to significant foreign exchange headwinds impacting its revenue. While sales grew in local currencies across most regions, a struggling Chinese property sector and a strong Swiss franc led to a decline in reported revenue, falling below analyst expectations.

news.title: Dubai home prices record first decline after post-pandemic boom
news.para: Dubai's red-hot housing market is cooling for the first time since 2020, with prices and sales volume dropping in March. Regional conflict is impacting demand, though developers are offering incentives. Despite jitters, some see end-users driving continued, albeit slower, sales, with a focus on long-term resilience.

news.title: Delhi goverment to introduce smart cards, cess portal to benefit construction workers
news.para: The Delhi government is set to launch innovative chip-enabled smart cards designed specifically for construction workers. This initiative will streamline the delivery of welfare schemes and simplify the registration process. Alongside this, a new digital portal is being developed to optimize cess collection from builders.

news.title: PwC Hong Kong to pay Evergrande shareholders HK$1 billion in compensation: SFC
news.para: Hong Kong's securities regulator has ordered PwC Hong Kong to compensate Evergrande's minority shareholders with HK$1 billion. This unprecedented settlement highlights significant professional misconduct by PwC, linked to the misleading financial reports of Evergrande from 2019 and 2020. The regulator is steadfast in its mission to safeguard investors and uphold the integrity of the financial markets.

news.title: Aurum PropTech posts net profit of ₹72 lakh in FY26
news.para: Aurum PropTech achieved a profit of ₹72 lakh in FY26, a major shift from its FY25 loss. Total income rose by over 50%. The company surpassed ₹500 crore ARR.  Q4 FY26 also showed strong performance with a profit of ₹16.44 crore.

news.title: Birla Estates reports booking value of ₹8,136 crore in FY26
news.para: Birla Estates reported booking value of ₹8,136 crore in FY26, with NCR emerging as the largest contributor, followed by Bengaluru and Mumbai. Collections rose 23.5% year-on-year.
